# Beudox HR - Technical Context for AI Assistants

## Overview
Beudox HR is a comprehensive Human Resources Management System built as a React TypeScript application with Supabase backend. It serves as a SaaS platform for organizations to manage their HR operations digitally.

## Architecture

### Frontend Architecture
- **Framework**: React 18 with TypeScript
- **Build Tool**: Vite with SWC for fast compilation
- **Routing**: React Router DOM with protected routes and role-based access control
- **State Management**: TanStack Query for server state, React Context for auth state
- **UI Components**: shadcn/ui built on Radix UI primitives with Tailwind CSS
- **Forms**: React Hook Form with Zod schema validation
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS with custom design tokens and CSS variables

### Backend Architecture
- **Platform**: Supabase (PostgreSQL database + Auth + Edge Functions)
- **Database**: PostgreSQL with migrations for schema management
- **Authentication**: Supabase Auth with custom user roles and permissions
- **API**: Supabase client for database operations, Edge Functions for complex business logic
- **File Storage**: Supabase Storage for documents and assets

### Key Design Patterns
- **Component Composition**: Extensive use of compound components and render props
- **Custom Hooks**: Business logic abstracted into reusable hooks (useAuth, useSort, etc.)
- **Provider Pattern**: Context providers for global state (Auth, QueryClient, Tooltip)
- **Protected Routes**: Route-level authentication and authorization
- **Role-Based Access**: Granular permissions system with role checking

## Core Features & Data Flow

### Authentication & Authorization
- Supabase Auth handles login/logout with email/password
- Custom roles system (admin, manager, employee, etc.)
- Route protection with `canAccess()` function checking user roles against route permissions
- Password reset and invite flows with URL hash token detection

### Employee Management
- CRUD operations for employee records
- Profile tabs: Attendance, Leave, Payroll, Documents, Reviews
- Searchable employee selection component with avatar display
- Employee lifecycle management (hire, promote, terminate)

### Attendance System
- Daily attendance records with check-in/check-out times
- Overtime calculation (regular + holiday OT)
- Status tracking (present, absent, late, weekend, holiday)
- Monthly summary with working days and OT hours

### Leave Management
- Leave type configuration (vacation, sick, personal)
- Leave balance tracking and automatic updates
- Request/approval workflow
- Calendar integration for leave planning

### Payroll Processing
- Automated payroll generation based on attendance and salary data
- Payslip generation and distribution
- Salary history and increment tracking
- Tax and deduction calculations

### Project Management
- Project creation with team assignments
- Task tracking and progress monitoring
- Activity logging for project updates
- Client association and billing

### Evaluation System
- Regular performance reviews with scheduled dates
- Daily evaluation system for ongoing feedback
- Review cycle management with increment proposals
- Evaluation parameters and scoring

## Key Files & Their Purposes

### Core Application Files
- `src/main.tsx`: Application entry point, renders App component
- `src/App.tsx`: Main app component with routing and providers
- `src/pages/Index.tsx`: Root page component (likely redirects)

### Authentication
- `src/hooks/useAuth.tsx`: Authentication hook managing session, user data, and roles
- `src/lib/role-access.ts`: Role-based access control utilities

### Components
- `src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx`: Main layout with sidebar and topbar
- `src/components/layout/AppSidebar.tsx`: Navigation sidebar with menu items
- `src/components/employee-profile/AttendanceTab.tsx`: Attendance records display
- `src/components/SearchableEmployeeSelect.tsx`: Employee selection dropdown
- `src/components/ui/`: shadcn/ui component library

### Utilities
- `src/lib/utils.ts`: Common utility functions (cn for class merging)
- `src/lib/format-date.ts`: Date formatting helpers
- `src/lib/leave-utils.ts`: Leave calculation and balance management
- `src/lib/review-schedule.ts`: Review scheduling logic

### Integrations
- `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`: Supabase client configuration
- `src/integrations/supabase/types.ts`: Generated TypeScript types from database schema

### Database
- `supabase/migrations/`: SQL migration files for database schema
- `supabase/functions/`: Edge functions for server-side logic (payroll generation, notifications)

## Business Logic Patterns

### Data Fetching
- TanStack Query for caching and synchronization
- Query keys follow pattern: ['entity', id, filters]
- Optimistic updates for better UX

### Form Handling
- React Hook Form for form state management
- Zod schemas for validation
- Controlled components with proper error handling

### Date/Time Handling
- date-fns for date manipulation and formatting
- Consistent date formats across the application
- Timezone handling for attendance records

### Notification System
- Toast notifications for user feedback
- Email notifications via Supabase Edge Functions
- In-app notification preferences

## Development Workflow

### Code Quality
- ESLint for code linting with React and TypeScript rules
- TypeScript strict mode enabled
- Prettier for code formatting (implied by ESLint config)

### Testing
- Vitest for unit testing
- Playwright for E2E testing
- React Testing Library for component testing

### Build & Deployment
- Vite for fast development and optimized production builds
- Environment variables for configuration
- Supabase CLI for database management

## Common Patterns & Conventions

### Component Props
- Interface definitions for all component props
- Optional props with sensible defaults
- Destructuring with default values

### Styling
- Tailwind CSS classes with custom design tokens
- CSS variables for theme colors (--bx-success-text, etc.)
- Consistent spacing and typography scales

### Error Handling
- Try/catch blocks around async operations
- User-friendly error messages via toast notifications
- Loading states with skeleton components

### Performance
- React.memo for expensive components
- useMemo/useCallback for computed values and event handlers
- Lazy loading for route components

This technical context should help AI assistants understand the codebase structure, patterns, and conventions used in Beudox HR development.
